workflow_name: QBR Transcript -> Health + Whitespace + Renewal Risk
user_message: Give me the manager view on this Pacific Northwest Outfitters support ticket. This is time-sensitive -- customer is waiting.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: executive brief Discussion - Pacific Northwest Outfitters
Date: 2024-01-21
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft), Kevin (Pacific Northwest Outfitters)

[00:01:15] Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is executive brief.

[00:08:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

[00:11:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about technical validation. Can we address those?

[00:16:00] Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about technical validation. Can we address those?

[00:21:00] Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about cost optimization. Can we address those?

[00:25:00] Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF45-L failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF45-L failure mode (Health score framework treats verbal feedback symmetrically when customer cultur)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct executive brief decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
